paragraphs.text: Bitcoin was created in 2008 by an anonymous person or group using the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto. The whitepaper titled "Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System" introduced a decentralized digital currency that operates without central banks or intermediaries.

concepts.label: Bitcoin operates without central banks or intermediaries
concepts.searchQuery: Does Bitcoin operate without central banks or intermediaries?
concepts.reason: Describes a defining feature of Bitcoin; worth confirming with reputable sources.
surfaceForms: operates without central banks or intermediaries

concepts.label: Bitcoin was created in 2008 by Satoshi Nakamoto

concepts.reason: Factual claim about the creation date and creator of Bitcoin.
surfaceForms: Bitcoin was created in 2008 by an anonymous person or group using the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto

concepts.label: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System
concepts.reason: This is a widely recognized whitepaper in cryptocurrency history.
surfaceForms: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System

concepts.label: Satoshi Nakamoto
concepts.reason: Identifies the creator(s) of Bitcoin; their identity is a major topic and is not definitively established.

concepts.label: Blockchain Technology
concepts.reason: Header introduces 'Blockchain Technology' as a domain-specific concept worth extraction and grounding.

paragraphs.text: A blockchain is a distributed ledger that records all transactions across a network of computers. Each block contains a cryptographic hash of the previous block, creating an immutable chain. Bitcoin's blockchain processes roughly 7 transactions per second, while Visa handles around 24,000.

concepts.label: Bitcoin's blockchain processes 7 transactions per second
concepts.reason: Quantitative performance metric for Bitcoin's blockchain.
surfaceForms: Bitcoin's blockchain processes roughly 7 transactions per second

concepts.label: Visa handles 24,000 transactions per second
concepts.reason: Quantifiable performance claim about Visa's transaction speed.

concepts.label: cryptographic hash creates immutable chain
concepts.searchQuery: how cryptographic hash creates immutable chain in blockchain
concepts.reason: Verifiable claim linking cryptographic hash to blockchain immutability.
surfaceForms: cryptographic hash of the previous block

concepts.label: blockchain definition
concepts.reason: Explicit definition provided for what a blockchain is.
surfaceForms: A blockchain is a distributed ledger that records all transactions across a network of computers

paragraphs.text: The consensus mechanism Bitcoin uses is called Proof of Work, where miners compete to solve complex mathematical puzzles. The first miner to solve the puzzle adds the next block and receives newly minted bitcoin as a reward. This reward started at 50 BTC and halves approximately every four years.

concepts.label: reward started at 50 BTC and halves approximately every four years
concepts.searchQuery: Bitcoin block reward history and halving interval
concepts.reason: A quantitative, verifiable claim about the Bitcoin block reward and its halving schedule.
surfaceForms: reward started at 50 BTC and halves approximately every four years

concepts.label: miners compete to solve complex mathematical puzzles
concepts.reason: Describes the core process in Proof of Work systems; central to Bitcoin mining.
surfaceForms: miners compete to solve complex mathematical puzzles

concepts.label: Proof of Work
concepts.reason: A well-known consensus mechanism in blockchain, worth grounding as a key term.

concepts.label: Ethereum and Smart Contracts
concepts.reason: Header introduces key terms that are likely central to the section; good for grounding.

paragraphs.text: Ethereum was proposed by Vitalik Buterin in 2013 and launched in 2015. It introduced smart contracts—self-executing programs stored on the blockchain that automatically enforce agreements when conditions are met.

paragraphs.text: In September 2022, Ethereum transitioned from Proof of Work to Proof of Stake in an event called "The Merge." This reduced Ethereum's energy consumption by approximately 99.95% according to the Ethereum Foundation.

concepts.label: The Merge reduced Ethereum's energy consumption by approximately 99.95% according to the Ethereum Foundation

concepts.reason: This is a specific, quantitative claim attributed to the Ethereum Foundation.
surfaceForms: reduced Ethereum's energy consumption by approximately 99.95% according to the Ethereum Foundation

paragraphs.text: DeFi applications allow users to lend, borrow, and trade without traditional financial intermediaries. The total value locked in DeFi protocols peaked at over $180 billion in late 2021 before declining significantly.

paragraphs.text: Uniswap, launched in 2018, pioneered the automated market maker model where liquidity providers deposit token pairs into pools. The protocol has processed over $1.8 trillion in trading volume since its launch.

concepts.reason: Header introduces the domain-specific concept of regulatory landscape, which may need grounding in legal or policy contexts.

paragraphs.text: The SEC has classified most cryptocurrencies as securities, with notable exceptions for Bitcoin and Ethereum. The landmark SEC v. Ripple case in 2023 established that programmatic sales of tokens on exchanges do not necessarily constitute securities transactions.

concepts.reason: Specific finding from SEC v. Ripple case, verification and expansion benefit from authoritative reference.
